title: Understand: galaxies and gravity
description: Check your understanding of galaxies and gravity in this set of free, standards-aligned practice questions.
topic_path: Middle school Earth and space science - NGSS

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which sequence correctly lists the celestial objects from smallest to largest?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Earth $\rightarrow$ galaxy $\rightarrow$ solar system $\rightarrow$ galaxy group
Earth $\rightarrow$ solar system $\rightarrow$ galaxy $\rightarrow$ galaxy group
galaxy group $\rightarrow$ galaxy $\rightarrow$ solar system $\rightarrow$ Earth
galaxy group $\rightarrow$ solar system $\rightarrow$ galaxy $\rightarrow$ Earth

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which set of celestial objects is correctly arranged from smallest to largest?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Local Group, Milky Way, solar system, Earth
Local Group, solar system, Milky Way, Earth
Earth, Milky Way, solar system, Local Group
Earth, solar system, Milky Way, Local Group

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which set of celestial objects is correctly arranged from smallest to largest?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: galaxy group, solar system, galaxy, Earth
galaxy group, galaxy, solar system, Earth
Earth, solar system, galaxy, galaxy group
Earth, galaxy, solar system, galaxy group

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the best option for the blank.**

>The force of gravity is $\_\_\_\_\_\_$ between celestial objects that have more mass.

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: stronger
weaker

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the best option for the blank.**

>The force of gravity is $\_\_\_\_\_\_$ between celestial objects that have less mass.

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: weaker
stronger

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the best option for the blank.**

>The force of gravity is stronger between celestial objects that have $\_\_\_\_\_\_$ mass.

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: more
less

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the best option for the blank.**

>The force of gravity is $\_\_\_\_\_\_$ between celestial objects that are closer together.

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: stronger
weaker

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the best option for the blank.**

>The force of gravity is $\_\_\_\_\_\_$ between celestial objects that are farther apart.

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: weaker
stronger

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the best option for the blank.**

>The force of gravity is stronger between celestial objects that are $\_\_\_\_\_\_$.


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: closer together
farther apart

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement about gravity is true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Gravity is a pulling force.
Gravity exists between some, but not all, celestial objects.
Gravity depends on both mass and shape.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement about gravity is true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Gravity is a force of attraction.
Gravity exists between some, but not all, celestial objects.
Gravity depends on both shape and distance.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement about gravity is true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Gravity exists between all celestial objects.
Gravity is a pushing force.
Gravity depends on both distance and shape.
